query.original: Can a grappled creature cast spells?
query.used: Can a grappled creature cast spells?

retrieved_nodes.preview: - You can use your action to try to pin a creature grappled by you.

## Cast a Spell

Spellcasters such as wizards and clerics, as well as many monsters, have access to spells and can use them to great effect in combat.

They might turn an enemy into a harmless creature, bolster the strength of an ally, make an object move at the caster's command, or enhance a creature's innate healing abilities to rapidly recover fro
Such spells can make enemies see the caster as a friend, force creatures to take a course of action, or even control another creature like a puppet.

It can innately cast the following spells, requiring only verbal Components

At will: *detect evil and good*, *detect magic*, *detect thoughts*

3/day each: *bless*, *create food and water*, *cure wou
Some spells can target only a creature (including you) that you touch.
Remember that you have disadvantage on a ranged attack roll if you are within 5 feet of a hostile creature that can see you and that isn't incapacitated.
You use the hand's Strength score to resolve the grapple.
Unless a spell has a perceptible effect, a creature might not know it was targeted by a spell at all.
Some characters and monsters have special abilities that let them cast spells without using spell slots.
